Title: v0.2.6 — Manifest contract + godot-lib (Genericization Phase 1)
Summary: First release of the v0.2.6 → v0.3.0 genericization arc. Adds godot-lib — a small Godot 4 / C# companion plugin you drop into your project — that emits a JSON manifest describing your project's BleepforgeResource subclasses. Bleepforge reads + validates the manifest in a new Diagnostics → Manifest tab. v0.2.7 will use it to drive generic editor surfaces; v0.2.6 is the foundation, NO UI changes to existing FoB workflows. Also: full project licensing (Apache 2.0 lib + AGPL v3 editor + TRADEMARK.md), packaging seed-path fix carried over from v0.2.5.
Body:
## The headline

Bleepforge's seven hardcoded game-domain editors (Items, Quests, NPCs, Karma, Factions, Dialogs, Balloons) work today against Yehonatan's Flock of Bleeps schema. The long-term direction is **generic** — the editor edits any 2D Godot 4 / C# project's content, with no per-domain editor code required.

v0.2.6 is the first of four releases on that arc:

- **v0.2.6** (this release) — Manifest contract + godot-lib library + editor reads manifest. **Editor UI does NOT change.** FoB workflow stays exactly as before. Foundation.
- **v0.2.7** — Generic editor surfaces (`<DomainList>`, `<DomainEdit>`) + override mechanism so bespoke FoB UIs keep working. Generic `.tres` mapper for writeback. Bleepforge edits any project that has a manifest.
- **v0.2.8** — Schema authoring + C# stub generation. Define new domains in Bleepforge, library generates C# stubs into your Godot project, you wire runtime logic via `partial class`, manifest re-exports, Bleepforge sees it.
- **v0.3.0** — Headline cut. Polish, docs, the advertise-able "Bleepforge is generic" release.

## What landed in v0.2.6

### The manifest contract — `bleepforge_manifest.json`

A JSON file that lives at your Godot project's root (when your project uses godot-lib). Describes every `BleepforgeResource` subclass: which of the four entry kinds it represents (`domain` / `discriminatedFamily` / `foldered` / `enumKeyed`), what fields it has across the 12 supported types, the editor surface it wants (`list` / `cards` / `graph`), any bespoke UI override.

Locked schema in [shared/src/manifest.ts](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/shared/src/manifest.ts) (zod). The Phase 0 audit walked every FoB schema + per-domain mapper and confirmed the 11-type catalog covers every authored field, with two minimal expansions (`subresource` for single inline sub-resources like NPC LootTable; `array.itemRef` for ref arrays like NPC CasualRemarks).

See [The Bleepforge manifest contract](/help/under-the-hood/manifest-contract) for the full design.

### godot-lib — the Godot companion plugin

A small C# plugin that lives at [godot-lib/addons/bleepforge/](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/tree/main/godot-lib/addons/bleepforge) in the Bleepforge monorepo. Two responsibilities:

**5 base classes** (`runtime/`) that handle the "walk a folder of `.tres`, index by key, expose O(1) lookup" pattern Godot projects (FoB included) typically write by hand for each domain:

- `BleepforgeResource` — marker base, every authored Resource subclasses this.
- `BleepforgeRegistry<T>` — generic flat registry (`domain` kind).
- `BleepforgeFolderedRegistry<T>` — composite-id grouping (`foldered` kind).
- `BleepforgeDiscriminatedRegistry<TBase>` — variant routing (`discriminatedFamily` kind).
- `BleepforgeEnumRegistry<TEnum, T>` — one-per-enum-value (`enumKeyed` kind).

Each has editor-time hot reload via `EditorInterface.Singleton.GetResourceFilesystem().FilesystemChanged` when the user adds `[Tool]` to their concrete subclass.

**A manifest emitter** (`editor/ManifestEmitter.cs`) that reflects over registry subclasses + their resource types' `[Export]` fields, discovers sub-resources transitively, and writes `bleepforge_manifest.json` at the project root. Two triggers wired today: editor-load auto-export + a `Tools → Re-export Bleepforge manifest` menu item.

Field types are inferred from C# type + `[Export]` hints unambiguously where possible (Texture2D → texture, PackedScene → scene, enum → enum, BleepforgeResource subclass → ref, etc.). The few cases the type system can't express get covered by 7 user attributes (`[BleepforgeFlag]`, `[BleepforgeShowWhen]`, `[BleepforgeNullable]`, `[BleepforgeArrayContainer]`, `[BleepforgeView]`, `[BleepforgeOverrideUi]`, `[BleepforgeDomain]`).

See [godot-lib — the companion library](/help/under-the-hood/godot-lib) for install + usage.

### Bleepforge reads + validates the manifest — Diagnostics → Manifest tab

Server-side loader at `server/src/lib/manifest/loader.ts` reads the manifest from the active project's Godot root and validates it against the shared zod schema. Returns a four-state result: `ok` / `missing` / `error` / `not-applicable`.

New **Manifest tab** in Diagnostics surfaces the parse state:

- **OK** (green) — schema version, file metadata, per-domain table (domain | kind | class | key | fields | view | overrideUi).
- **Not detected** (amber) — normal for projects without godot-lib installed; pointer at install instructions.
- **Error** (red) — parse / validation failure with per-issue list.
- **Not applicable** (muted) — notebook-mode projects (no Godot root by design).

The Manifest tab contributes severity to the Diagnostics header icon ONLY on `error` (warning level) — `missing` and `not-applicable` are normal cases and don't surface as problems.

**No UI changes to FoB workflows.** Existing Items, Quests, NPCs, etc. surfaces work exactly as before. The manifest tab is purely informational in v0.2.6.

### Validation harness — godot-lib/test-project

Synthetic Godot 4.4+ project at [godot-lib/test-project/](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/tree/main/godot-lib/test-project) that exercises godot-lib + the manifest emitter against all four entry kinds:

- `Note` (domain) — flat folder, key=Slug.
- `Snippet` (foldered) — groupBy=ParentDir (language token).
- `ElementData` (enumKeyed) — 4 enum values: Fire/Water/Earth/Air.
- `Equipment` (discriminatedFamily) — base + Sword/Shield variants.

13 sample `.tres` files. Setup script (`./setup.sh`) symlinks the addon into the test project so changes to godot-lib propagate without copying. Walkthrough in the [test-project README](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/godot-lib/test-project/README.md).

Use it to verify the round-trip end-to-end: open in Godot → manifest emits → open in Bleepforge → Manifest tab shows 4 domains green.

## Project licensing

Bleepforge had no LICENSE up to v0.2.5 (technically all-rights-reserved by default). v0.2.6 ships a deliberate split:

- **godot-lib** (the companion library) — **Apache License 2.0**. Permissive for adoption. Stronger than MIT: explicit "no trademark license granted" + patent grant.
- **The editor** (everything else in this repo) — **GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later**. Copyleft: forks must remain open AGPL, including SaaS hosts. Removes the financial incentive to rebadge-and-monetize.
- **TRADEMARK.md** — "Bleepforge" name + logo are unregistered marks of Yehonatan Moravia. Forks welcome under the licenses; use a different name. Descriptive use ("compatible with Bleepforge") and fair-use reference allowed.

Apache 2.0 + AGPL v3 are both OSI-approved open-source licenses. Premium tier-2 add-ons (deferred indefinitely) would ship as separate proprietary products.

## Packaging fix

**Help library seed path** — fixed a v0.2.5 leftover where `electron-builder.json` pointed at `../data/help` (a path the multi-project migration emptied). Now points at `../projects/flock-of-bleeps/data/help`. AppImage + Windows installer builds will correctly bundle the Help seed again. Long-term we should hoist the seed source out of the per-project tree (probably v0.2.7+).

## Known issues / deferred

- **Variant default-value conflict in discriminatedFamily emit.** When the emitter detects variant values for a discriminated family, it instantiates each variant via `Activator.CreateInstance` and reads the discriminator field's default. But variant subclasses inherit their default from the base, so all variants get the enum's first value as their `value` in the manifest. Worked around in the test project by setting the discriminator in each variant's constructor. Long-term fix: a `[BleepforgeVariantValue("...")]` attribute on the variant class. v0.2.7 candidate.
- **Build hook trigger** (CI pre-build emit without opening editor) deferred. Editor-load + manual menu cover the primary workflow.
- **The `enum` type is read-only in v0.2.6.** Values come from C# enum reflection; you can't add a new enum value from inside Bleepforge. v0.2.8's schema authoring will add a `valuesEditable: bool` flag for Bleepforge-authored enums.
- **No SSE / live update on manifest changes** in v0.2.6. The Manifest tab refreshes on demand. v0.2.7's generic surfaces will need live updates and can extend the existing watcher infrastructure.
- **First-install C# build dance** (build C# manually, then enable plugin) — Godot 4 platform limitation, documented in both READMEs and in the godot-lib Help entry. Subsequent opens work normally.

## What's next (v0.2.7)

- Generic `<DomainList>` + `<DomainEdit>` driven by manifest field declarations.
- Generic `.tres` mapper for writeback (the meaty bit — sub-resource positional reconcile, ext-resource minting, default-aware per-field, AtlasTexture preservation).
- Override mechanism so bespoke FoB UIs (DialogGraph, NpcEdit with loot+quests, BalloonCard) keep working via the `overrideUi` field.
- Generic graph view for any domain with `view: "graph"` + a ref field.
- The `[BleepforgeVariantValue]` attribute mentioned above.

v0.2.7 = "Bleepforge edits any project that has a manifest."

> note: the architectural deep-dive lives in the **Genericization arc** section of the project's CLAUDE.md. Read there for the locked design decisions, the riskiest call across the arc (the v0.2.7 generic mapper), and the mapper-audit-driven mitigation strategy.
